title: valerianella grayi
intro: Valerianella grayi, also known as Gray's Corn Spurrey, is a low-growing, annual herb native to the Iberian Peninsula and Mediterranean regions. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Valerianella grayi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can be dried or used in capsules for its medicinal properties.
